notes: Couple owning home outright
Location-specific red flag none of the other three Belize places carry
A specific, practical reason expats choose Corozal — cross-border access to Mexican medical care and shopping, distinct from the national Belize City/Belmopan-only picture above
Small absolute numbers, but a real uptick, not zero

value_raw: Smaller than San Pedro's or San Ignacio's
notes: No hard headcount found for Corozal specifically, unlike San Pedro (largest, most established) — a real, sourced relative ranking, not a precise number
The lowest-priced realistic purchase tier found anywhere in Belize, still the low/fixer end
Cross-checked across 3 sites (willmitchellrealestate.com, properstar.com, alpharealestatebelize.com)

value_raw: Quieter and thinner, tied to a useful binational routine
notes: Explicitly not framed as either a rich social scene or a social desert — a distinct, practical draw (see the Chetumal-proximity row above) rather than a community-density pitch
Repealed 2001, producing the current full-freehold regime.

fact_label: Annual property tax (unimproved land value only)
notes: Typical bill $100-200/yr on a developed 4BR home — cross-checked across a 9-year source gap

fact_label: Default evacuation destination for serious/complex emergencies
notes: New this pass; bz.usembassy.gov, expatfinancial.com, 2026 — distinct from the shorter Chetumal/Mérida cross-border trip already documented for routine specialist care below

fact_label: Digital Nomad/Long Stay permit requires proof of health insurance
notes: The one Belize route that does mandate coverage — already a non-fit on income grounds ($75,000/year) regardless

value_raw: Concentrated in Belize City/Belmopan; no oncology or endocrinology capacity nationally
notes: Karl Heusner Memorial Hospital (public referral) and Belize Medical Associates (leading private) are the national anchors; complex specialty cases require leaving the country

fact_label: Foreigner access route into the public healthcare system
value_raw: Yes, via SSB registration + BHIS card — same terms as citizens, 'very low-cost and almost free'
notes: New this pass, a genuine positive not previously documented; expatfinancial.com + jarniascyril.com, 2026, independently converging. Gated on SSB/BHIS registration, not open to an unregistered visitor; real trade-offs — long queues, diagnostics billed separately even in the public tier — are why most expats still choose private care despite technical access

value_raw: 1 year continuous legal residence (max 14 days absence) + a discretionary 'financially stable' standard, no fixed dollar figure
notes: Spot-check finding: a real second viable route beyond the Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP), distinct from Options 2/4/5 (Long Stay Permit, Visitor's Permit, investment-residence), not evaluated further elsewhere. Belize's own Department of Immigration site is the official source; a genuinely different shape than every other candidate's routes in this table — no fixed income number at all, a discretionary standard instead. No criminal conviction carrying 12+ months' potential imprisonment, in any country

How exactly someone without the Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) or Long Stay Permit eligibility legally accumulates a full continuous year of "residing in Belize" to even apply under this route was not fully resolved in the source file's own pass — named there as "the single highest-value question to answer before treating this as a real fallback, not just a theoretical one"
The 'financially stable' standard is discretionary and doesn't name an income type at all — genuinely unresolved (checked-and-silent), not a guessed Yes/No.

fact_label: General (non-QRP) PR requires proof of health insurance
notes: immigration.gov.bz, 2026 — the "financially stable" standard is proven via bank statements, not an insurance policy

A real, structural, area-specific planning constraint for coastal/caye property specifically — not yet broken out with dated storm-history detail the way Asheville's Helene or Rincón's Fiona findings are
expatlife.ai, internationalinsurance.com, 2026; varies by age and coverage level — see the age-ceiling row above

Not specifically searched for at the national or any candidate-area level — distinct from the already-checked "no organized anti-foreigner backlash" finding (community-network.md), which is a social-movement question, not a criminal-targeting one
Divergence verdict now recorded directly in the red-flags research file itself, not only in the scoring rationale

New this pass; medical-air-service.com, airambulanceworldwide.com, travelcareair.com, 2026 — no Belize-specific fixed figure, broadly convergent range. Real uninsured tail risk given neither the Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) nor general PR mandates coverage against it
Worse than Guatemala's national rate, roughly comparable to Colombia's large-city rates

fact_label: No capital gains tax on property sale
notes: Also restated in property.md's Legal structure section
Downstream of the dog-import gap above

fact_label: Pet import (dog) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
notes: Not yet researched for Belize
expatlife.ai, internationalinsurance.com, 2026

value_raw: Provider-dependent: IMG Global caps new enrollment at 65 (existing members move to a Global Senior plan at 75); Cigna Global states no upper age limit
notes: New this pass — not a uniform "65-75" rule, genuinely provider-specific, checked directly against IMG's and Cigna Global's own sites rather than assumed

value_raw: ~8 major public hospitals, ~60 public clinics; underfunded, understaffed, under-supplied outside Belize City
notes: expatfinancial.com, 2026

fact_label: Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) application-fraud lived account
notes: Named first-hand account: a local agent for the Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) lost an application fee, forged a signature, never submitted the application

fact_label: Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) converts to permanent residency
notes: Does not feed Belize's general PR track; sole named exception: US$500,000 investor contribution. Belize's own file states the Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) functions as a standalone 'permanent residency equivalent to a green card' and, explicitly, that QRP time does NOT accrue toward general PR ('For the vast majority of folks who will join QRP, they will not earn time accrued in Belize toward Permanent Residency') — a bare 'Yes' misleadingly implied this route feeds Belizean PR; it does not, it is its own indefinite status.

fact_label: Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) income-type requirement (passive)
notes: BTB's own official program page (belizetourismboard.org/programs-events/retirement-program/, direct fetch 2026-07-12, primary-institutional) names 'Pension, Annuity, Social Security Benefits, Investment Account, Inheritance, Reverse Mortgage, Personal Savings, Retirement Contribution Plans, and other means of retirement income' as qualifying — materially broader than lawbelize.bz's narrower 'pension or annuity' framing this row previously carried. Not fully closed to the last degree: whether rental income specifically, vs. a brokerage/investment account, is independently accepted, is unconfirmed.

fact_label: Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) retirement-visa income threshold
notes: Stated threshold; divergence flag confirmed via a named fraud account involving a Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) agent (below)

fact_label: Qualified Retired Persons program (QRP) visa requires proof of health insurance
notes: lawbelize.bz, direct fetch, full requirement list checked — the compound age-gated-insurance gate this criterion was built to catch does not bite on Belize's principal route, a real checked result
Full-freehold regime applies even at Corozal's Mexico-border position, confirmed for all four candidate areas, not just assumed
expatsi.com, riviera-expat.com, 2026; complex procedures routinely outsourced to Mexico — the (a) system-capability sub-fact the new criterion's definition names explicitly

fact_label: Stamp duty on purchase (non-CARICOM foreign buyer)
notes: 5% for Belizean/CARICOM nationals; first ~$10,000 often exempt
Starlink licensing fight unresolved — a contested trajectory
Attorney fees typically 1-2%; includes the 8% non-CARICOM stamp duty already extracted as its own row.

value_raw: 2:1 fixed peg since 1976, ~4.5 months import cover, reserves up 15% YoY
notes: One of the longest-running fixed pegs in the Western Hemisphere; Coface's country-risk analysis states the peg is "not at risk" on current reserve strength. Central Bank's precise reserve-to-liability ratio not independently confirmed — directional read only, not a confirmed comfortable margin

value_raw: Incumbent PUP won 26 of 31 seats; PM John Briceño returned for a second consecutive (8th personal) term on a policy-continuation platform
notes: sanpedrosun.com, nationalassembly.gov.bz, Wikipedia — no regime change, no populist-rupture platform, no currency/capital-control reversal signal in the campaign or post-election agenda. Tracy Panton became the first female opposition leader, a real institutional first but not one that changes currency, investment, or tax posture

value_raw: New 2026 fast-track to permanent residency for foreign nationals committing $500,000+ to commercial ventures, faster than the standard one-year-residence route; no rollback of the post-2001 full-freehold foreign-ownership regime found
notes: imidaily.com, the-immigration.com; US State Department's 2025 Investment Climate Statement confirms no restrictions on foreign ownership/control of companies, naming only "bureaucratic and regulatory challenges" as friction, not a rules-changing threat

unit: % of GDP (61.1 in 2024, down from a 2020 peak of 103.3)
notes: IMF's 2025 Article IV consultation concluded cooperatively, welcoming Belize's own commitment to bring debt below 50% of GDP by 2030. S&P affirmed Belize's B-/B sovereign credit rating in 2024 — still solidly speculative-grade, not a strong-institutions signal on its own. Primary fiscal balance narrowing (1.7% of GDP FY2024, projected 1.1% FY2025, 0.8% FY2026) — a real trend worth tracking, not a crisis but not nothing either

value_raw: Many ordinary Belizeans, especially rural/indigenous communities, lack formal title to land they live on and farm, even as government actively courts foreign real-estate capital
notes: thereporter.bz, built around an interview with PM Briceño himself — a real institutional-capacity/equity signal for judging overall governance quality, distinct from (and not a change to) the freehold mechanism foreign buyers themselves use
